title: Airbnb Q1 2025 Financial Results
text: Airbnb Q1 2025 revenue was $2.3 billion, up 6% year-over-year. Revenue increased to $2.3 billion in Q1 2025 from $2.1 billion in Q1 2024, primarily driven by solid growth in nights stayed, partially offset by a slight decrease in Average Daily Rate. When you exclude the impact of FX and calendar factors, revenue would have increased 11%. Earnings per share: 24 cents vs. 24 cents expected; Revenue: $2.27 billion vs. $2.26 billion expected. Gross booking value increased 7% year over year to $24.5 billion, in line with estimates. Nights and experiences booked rose 8% from a year ago and totaled 143.1 million, compared to the 143.4 million estimate from analysts. In Q1, guests spent nearly $25 billion on Airbnb. Q1 net income was $154 million, representing a 7% net income margin. Net income decreased to $154 million in Q1 2025 compared to $264 million in Q1 2024, primarily due to higher stock-based compensation expense largely driven by increased headcount. Q1 Adjusted EBITDA was $417 million, representing an 18% Adjusted EBITDA Margin. Q1 Free Cash Flow was $1.8 billion, representing a FCF Margin of 78%. The company's revenue forecast for the second quarter was weaker than analysts had expected. In the U.S., the company noted relatively softer results, which they believe has been largely driven by broader economic uncertainties.
